benchmark_v3_metadata.procedure_details: Sodium hydride (50% in mineral oil, 1.1 gms, 0.045 m) is added in portions to a stirred solution of trans 4-(p-tolylsulphonyl)-4,6,6a,8,9,10a hexahydro-7H-indolo-[3,4 gh]-[1,4] benzoxazin-8-one (7.0 gm, 0.018 m) in DMF (14 ml). The resulting mixture is stirred at room temperature for 1/2 hour. Methyl iodide (5.0 gm, 0.035 m) is added dropwise to the reaction mixture. After stirring an additional 1/2 hour, the reaction solution is poured into water, and the solid is removed by filtration. The tan solid is chromatographed (silica, chloroform) to give 3 gms of trans-4,6,6a,8,9,10a-Hexahydro-7-methyl-7H-indolo-[3,4 gh]-[1.4]-benzoxazine.
benchmark_v3_metadata.selection_note: ORDerly official retrosynthesis test split row.

benchmark_v3_metadata.procedure_details: A mixture of 5-amino-3-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-(pyridin-4-yl)pyrazole (100 mg) and 1,1-dicyano-2-ethoxyethylene (49 mg) in acetic acid (2 ml) was refluxed for 1 hour. The reaction mixture was concentrated in vacuo and the residue was crystallized from ethanol to give 7-amino-6-cyano-2-(4-fluorophenyl)-3-(pyridin-4-yl)pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine (77 mg).
benchmark_v3_metadata.selection_note: ORDerly official retrosynthesis test split row.

question_text:
Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O

benchmark_v3_gold.reference_answer: OC(CCC(O)=O)=O + C=CCO (2 equivalents) + TsOH = O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C; O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C + 1) NaH (2 equivalents), C=CCO, 2) HCl, H2O = OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1; OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1 + MeI (2 equivalents), K2CO3 (2 equivalents) = O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O.
